title: Polish Fruit Dumplings
description: A traditional Polish dessert known as 'Paczki' filled with sweet fruit and topped with a sweet icing. This recipe uses a fermented dough to give it a unique texture and flavor.

ingredients.name: Fermented dough (Paczki dough)
ingredients.notes: Available at most Polish markets or online

ingredients.name: Mixed fruit (cherries, blueberries, raspberries)
ingredients.notes: Can be adjusted to personal preference

ingredients.name: Polish mix (powdered sugar, cinnamon)
ingredients.notes: Available at most Polish markets or online

instructions: Mix the fermented dough according to the package instructions.
Roll out the dough to a thickness of about 1/4 inch.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a tablespoon of mixed fruit in the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the fruit to form a triangle and press the edges together to seal.
Bring a large pot of water to a boil and gently add the dumplings.
Cook the dumplings for 5-7 minutes or until they float to the surface.
Drain the dumplings and top with powdered sugar and cinnamon.
Optional: sprinkle with chewy sugar for extra sweetness.
